commands.repair_reason: The remaining work is contributor/maintainer validation for an open security-sensitive PR, not a source repair that ClawSweeper can safely create.; Cleared: The diff tightens Convex moderation-state checks and adds tests/spec coverage without adding dependencies, scripts, secret handling, workflow permissions, or package-resolution changes. commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Convex skill ban/unban batch handling, adds a focused test file, changelog entry, and moderation spec text to prevent stale unban restore pages from reactivating skills after a later ban or deactivation.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, not from a live run: current main restores user.banned rows without re-reading the owner and skips retimestamping already ban-hidden rows. That gives a clear path for a delayed unban page to match an old ban marker after the owner is banned or deactivated again.
commands.review_followup: The remaining work is contributor/maintainer validation for an open security-sensitive PR, not a source repair that ClawSweeper can safely create.

target.title: fix: abort stale unban skill restore batches
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens user-unban restore batches so stale pages cannot undo a later ban or deactivation.

## What changed

- Unban skill-restore pages abort when the user has already been banned/deactivated again.
- Later ban markers are monotonic; older delayed ban pages cannot roll `softDeletedAt` back from a newer ban timestamp.
- Removed rows are not retimestamped or restored.

## Behavior proof

The focused stale batch suite proves the restore boundary:

- A stale unban restore batch aborts after a later ban.
- A stale unban restore batch aborts after deactivation.
- Older delayed ban pages cannot overwrite a newer ban marker.
- Removed rows remain removed.

The intended behavior is fail-closed: stale pages abort instead of restoring old state.

## Validation

```text
$ bun run test convex/skills.banBatches.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       6 passed (6)
```

```text
$ bun run ci:static
passed
```

```text
$ bun run ci:unit
Test Files  205 passed (205)
Tests       1991 passed (1991)
Statements  85.98%
Branches    75.17%
Functions   86.37%
Lines        89.48%
```

```text
$ bun run ci:types-build
tsc, package typechecks, clawhub-mod typecheck, Vite build, and Nitro build passed.
```

## External blocker

The known Vercel preview failure is team authorization required for deployment. That is maintainer-owned and not a patch failure.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the diff, can maintain it, and validated it with the commands above.
